_prompt_meta.title: TalentGraph AI — Ultimate Real-World UI Specification
_prompt_meta.purpose: Drop this JSON into Cursor, Windsurf, v0.dev, or Lovable and get a production-ready enterprise SaaS UI with zero guesswork
_prompt_meta.target_stack: Next.js 15 App Router · TypeScript · Tailwind CSS v4 · shadcn/ui · Framer Motion · Recharts · TanStack Table v8 · Zustand · React Query v5
_prompt_meta.design_north_star: Every screen should feel like Linear meets Notion AI — information-dense but never claustrophobic, with real breathing room between every element. The UI should feel like it was built by a team of senior engineers at a well-funded Series A startup, not a template.

project.name: TalentGraph AI
project.tagline: Beyond Resume Matching. Predict Hiring Success.
project.product_description: TalentGraph AI is an enterprise talent intelligence platform. Recruiters paste a job description, upload up to 100 resumes, and get an AI-ranked shortlist in under 5 seconds — with an explainable score breakdown for every candidate. It replaces gut-feel screening with structured, auditable intelligence.

primary_persona.name: Sarah Chen
primary_persona.pain_point: Manually screening 80+ resumes per role takes 3–4 hours. She needs to shortlist 5 candidates for a hiring manager by tomorrow morning.

breathing_space.principle: White space is not empty space — it is the primary organizational tool. Every card, section, and element breathes. Nothing touches. Nothing crowds.
rules: Minimum 32px vertical padding inside every card
Minimum 16px between any label and its value
Section headers always have 8px of eyebrow text + 4px gap + heading + 8px gap before content
Tables have 20px vertical padding per row — never 12px or less
Modal content has 32px padding on all sides
Page content never touches the sidebar — always 24px gap

visual_hierarchy.principle: The eye must always know where to go next. One thing is the most important on every screen.

levels.L1_primary_action: One per screen. Full-width or prominent CTA. Blue, always visible.
levels.L2_primary_data: The number or status the user came to see. Large, high-contrast.
levels.L4_metadata: Timestamps, IDs, secondary labels. Text-muted, smallest size.
levels.L5_structural: Borders, dividers, backgrounds. Should recede completely.

real_world_ux.principle: This is a tool used by professionals under time pressure. Every interaction must be fast, predictable, and forgiving.
rules: No action requires more than 3 clicks from any screen
Destructive actions always have confirmation dialogs
All uploads show real progress — not spinners
All AI operations show real-time status — not just a loading screen
Search results appear within 200ms of typing (debounced 150ms)
Every table row is individually actionable without selecting it first
Back navigation always works and returns the user to exact scroll position

copy_principles.tone: Confident and direct. Written for busy professionals. No fluff, no jargon, no exclamation marks.
copy_principles.labels: Verb-first: 'Upload resumes' not 'Resume Upload'. 'Generate ranking' not 'AI Processing'.
copy_principles.empty_states: Always action-oriented: tell the user exactly what to do next.
copy_principles.errors: Always specific: 'PDF too large (12MB). Max file size is 10MB.' not 'Upload failed.'

colors._comment: The palette is cool-dark. Background is near-black navy, not pure black. Cards are slate with enough lightness to feel elevated. Only one warm accent (amber for warnings) — everything else is cool.

surface.note: Sidebar, secondary panels — one step above base

score_spectrum._comment: Used only for score rendering. Color is derived from score value, not hardcoded per candidate.

spacing._system: 4px base unit. All spacing is multiples of 4.

typography._system: Inter for all UI text. JetBrains Mono for scores, IDs, code. The type scale has 8 steps — never invent new sizes.

label.usage: Eyebrow labels, column headers — UPPERCASE only

elevation._system: Cards are elevated via border + subtle box-shadow. Never use backdrop-filter on primary cards — only on modals and dropdowns.
elevation.level_0: No shadow. Used for table rows, list items embedded in cards.

motion._principle: Motion must have purpose. Animate to guide attention, confirm action, or smooth a transition. Never animate to decorate.

easing.default: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)  — snappy ease-out for most transitions
easing.spring: cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) — slight overshoot for score ring, badges

patterns.card_entrance: opacity: 0→1, translateY: 12px→0 over 450ms with stagger: index * 50ms
patterns.score_ring: SVG stroke-dashoffset animates from full-circumference to target over 900ms
patterns.counter: requestAnimationFrame easeOut from 0 to target over 700ms
patterns.sidebar_collapse: width transition 250ms cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), text opacity 150ms 0ms→100ms delay
patterns.pipeline_step: checkmark scale 0→1 spring 300ms when step completes

motion.reduced_motion: All animations replaced with instant transitions when prefers-reduced-motion: reduce

page_shell.structure: Fixed sidebar (left) + Fixed header (top, right of sidebar) + Scrollable main content
page_shell.css_grid: display:grid; grid-template-columns: {sidebarWidth} 1fr; grid-template-rows: {headerHeight} 1fr; height: 100vh; overflow: hidden;
page_shell.main_scroll: The only scrollable element is the main content area — sidebar and header never scroll

logo_area.content: Logo SVG (24x24) + 'TalentGraph AI' wordmark in Inter 600 — fades to just icon on collapse

upgrade_banner.content: Upgrade to Pro — Unlock unlimited candidates

user_row.content: Avatar (32px circle) + Name (body_sm, text-primary) + Role (body_sm, text-muted)

collapse_toggle.position: absolute, right: -12px, top: 50% of nav section

breadcrumb.example: Dashboard  /  AI Ranking  /  Senior Frontend Engineer

search.style: bg-card border-default focus:border-blue-500 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500/20 rounded-lg
search.behavior: Focus opens command palette overlay with recent searches + quick results

items.dropdown: Notification panel slides in from right

screens.name: Dashboard — Home
screens.purpose: The recruiter's home base. Start a new ranking or pick up where you left off.
screens.breathing_notes: This screen should feel like arriving at a clean desk in the morning, not a cluttered command center. Hero section takes up 60% vertical height. Stats are secondary.

layout.type: Two-column on large screens, single column on mobile

content.headline: h1 — 'Good morning, Sarah.'
content.subtext: body_lg text-muted — 'You have 3 active jobs and 47 unreviewed candidates.'

left.heading: h3 — 'What role are you hiring for?'

right.button: Parse JD — outline sm — only visible after text is entered

textarea.placeholder: Paste the full job description here — title, responsibilities, required skills, experience level...
textarea.style: bg-transparent border border-default focus:border-primary focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary/15 rounded-lg p-4 font-body_md text-primary placeholder:text-muted w-full
textarea.character_counter: body_sm text-muted — '0 / 5000' — bottom right, visible on focus

parsed_skills_section.visibility: Only appears after JD is parsed

required_skills.label: Required skills
required_skills.style: bg-success-subtle text-success border border-success-border rounded-full px-3 py-1 text-body_sm font-medium

nice_to_have.label: Nice to have
nice_to_have.style: bg-info-subtle text-info border border-info-border rounded-full px-3 py-1 text-body_sm font-medium

header.heading: h3 — 'Upload candidate resumes'
header.subtext: body_sm text-muted — 'PDF or DOCX, up to 100 files, 10MB each'

idle.style: border-2 border-dashed border-slate-700 rounded-xl bg-card/40 p-10 text-center cursor-pointer hover:border-slate-500 hover:bg-card/60 transition-all 200ms
idle.heading: h4 text-secondary — 'Drop resumes here'
idle.subtext: body_sm text-muted mt-1 — 'or click to browse files'
idle.note: The drop zone has generous internal padding — 40px. It does not look cramped.

uploading.shows: Progress bar + '23 of 47 files uploaded...' in body_sm text-muted

file_list.visibility: Appears below drop zone after files are selected
file_list.overflow: scroll with fade mask at bottom
file_list.header: flex justify-between — 'h4: {n} files selected' + 'Clear all button ghost sm'

item.remove: X icon button ghost, 20px, appears on row hover

generate_button.style_ready: bg-primary text-white font-semibold shadow-[0_0_20px_rgba(37,99,235,0.3)] hover:bg-primary-hover hover:shadow-[0_0_28px_rgba(37,99,235,0.4)] transition-all 200ms

content.label: h4 — 'Generate AI Ranking'
content.subtext: body_sm opacity-70 ml-auto — 'Analyzes {n} resumes against your JD'

generate_button.keyboard_hint: ⌘ Return — shown on right edge in mono_sm text-muted

kpi_card_structure.trend_style: body_sm mt-1 — green if up, muted if neutral, red if down

empty_state.heading: h4 text-muted — 'No jobs yet'
empty_state.subtext: body_sm text-muted — 'Paste a job description on the left to start your first ranking.'

screens.name: AI Processing
screens.purpose: Full-screen pipeline view. The recruiter watches their resumes get analyzed in real time.
screens.breathing_notes: This screen is centered, quiet, and focused. Nothing distracts. The pipeline steps are the entire UI.

top_section.headline: h1 — 'Ranking your candidates'
top_section.subtext: body_lg text-muted mt-2 — 'Evaluating {n} resumes against Senior Frontend Engineer...'

pipeline_steps.connector_line: 2px solid — dashed if pending, solid if complete, blue if active

indicator.pending: bg-card border-2 border-default — icon inside: step number in mono_sm text-muted
indicator.complete: bg-success-subtle border-2 border-success — check icon inside (green 16px)

content.sublabel_active: body_sm text-muted mt-1 — real-time detail e.g. 'Found 14 required skills, 3 optional'

steps.label: Semantic skill matching
steps.sublabel: Comparing candidate skills to job requirements

steps.label: Analyzing career trajectory
steps.sublabel: Scoring growth, promotions, and tenure patterns

steps.label: Predicting hiring readiness
steps.sublabel: Checking for availability and interview signals

footer.cancel_link: body_sm text-muted underline hover:text-secondary cursor-pointer mt-3 text-center — 'Cancel and go back'

screens.name: Candidate Ranking Dashboard
screens.purpose: The main output. A ranked table of all candidates with scores, filters, and quick actions.
screens.breathing_notes: Table rows need room to breathe. 20px vertical padding per row minimum. The table should feel like a premium data tool, not a spreadsheet.

left.heading: h1 — 'Senior Frontend Engineer'
left.meta: body_md text-muted mt-1 — 'Acme Corp · 47 candidates analyzed · Ranked 4 minutes ago'

search.placeholder: Search by name, skill, or location...

active_filters.display: FilterChips appear here when filters are applied
active_filters.clear_all: Ghost button — 'Clear all' — appears only when filters are active

filter_button.label: Filters
filter_button.badge: {n} active filters badge when filters applied
filter_button.opens: Filter panel slides in from right (drawer)

candidate.avatar: 36x36 rounded-full bg-gradient-to-br from-primary to-secondary — initials in body_sm text-white font-semibold
candidate.name: body_md font-semibold text-primary
candidate.headline: body_sm text-muted — current title
candidate.spacing: Name and headline in a flex-col gap-0.5

score.component: ScoreBadge — pill with score number and color by range
score.style: score_md font-bold in rounded-full px-3 py-1

match.bar: w-20 h-1.5 rounded-full bg-border-default overflow-hidden — inner bar fills to match %

actions.layout: flex items-center gap-1 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 transition-opacity
actions.more: MoreHorizontal icon button → dropdown: [Compare, Shortlist, Download PDF, divider, Remove]

bulk_action_bar.trigger: Appears as fixed bottom bar when 1+ rows selected
bulk_action_bar.style: fixed bottom-6 left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 bg-card border border-default rounded-xl px-5 py-3 shadow-[0_8px_32px_rgba(0,0,0,0.5)] flex items-center gap-4
bulk_action_bar.content: body_sm font-medium — '{n} candidates selected' + divider + [Shortlist, Compare (max 3), Export, divider, Deselect all]

pagination.right: Prev / 1 2 3 / Next — numbered pagination with page size selector

filter_drawer.position: fixed right-0 top-0 h-full z-50 bg-surface border-l border-default shadow-level_4
filter_drawer.header: flex justify-between items-center px-6 py-5 border-b border-default

sections.label: LOCATION
sections.type: Tag input — type to add locations

filter_drawer.footer: px-6 py-4 border-t border-default — 'Reset filters' ghost + 'Apply filters' primary

screens.name: Candidate Profile
screens.purpose: Deep-dive on a single candidate. The recruiter decides whether to shortlist based on this screen.
screens.breathing_notes: Two-column layout. Left is the candidate's story (resume). Right is AI's verdict. Both columns have generous padding. Sections are separated by 40px not dividers.

back_nav.style: flex items-center gap-2 text-muted hover:text-secondary body_sm cursor-pointer mb-6 w-fit
back_nav.content: ChevronLeft 14px + 'Back to Senior Frontend Engineer results'

layout.type: Two-column, left scrolls, right is sticky

avatar.style: rounded-full bg-gradient-to-br from-primary to-secondary font-bold text-white text-2xl flex items-center justify-center

info.name: h1 — candidate full name
info.title: body_lg text-secondary mt-1 — 'Senior Full-Stack Engineer at Atlassian'
info.meta_row: flex items-center gap-4 mt-2 — [MapPin+location, Mail icon, Phone icon, LinkedIn icon]

header.left: flex items-center gap-2 — Sparkles 16px text-primary + h3 text-primary 'AI Executive Summary'

ai_executive_summary.content: body_lg text-secondary leading-relaxed — AI-generated 3-4 sentence paragraph
ai_executive_summary.real_example: Priya is an exceptionally strong match for this role. Her 6 years at high-growth B2B SaaS companies map directly to your stack, and her promotion from mid-level to senior in under 3 years signals above-average career velocity. Two gaps worth exploring in interview: system design at scale and Kubernetes ops experience.

left.logo: 40x40 rounded-lg bg-card border border-default flex items-center justify-center — company initial or logo

right.achievements: ul mt-3 space-y-2 — body_sm text-secondary with dot bullets
right.achievement_limit: Show 3, expand to all on 'Show more' link

ring.outer_ring: Overall score arc — 16px stroke, color by score range, animated draw
ring.inner_arcs: 6 thin arcs (4px stroke) arranged concentrically inside outer ring — each arc = one dimension
ring.center: score_xl text-primary — animated counter + body_sm text-muted 'Overall Score'

grade_badge.style: Pill badge large — e.g. 'A — Strong Match' in emerald

item.left: flex items-center gap-2 — [color dot 8px] + [Icon 14px text-muted] + body_sm text-secondary
item.right: flex items-center gap-3 — [mini progress bar w-20 h-1] + score_md font-bold text-{color}

chart.legend: flex items-center gap-4 mt-3 — colored dot + body_sm text-muted

skills_grid.matched_chip: bg-success-subtle text-success border border-success-border rounded-full px-3 py-1 body_sm font-medium
skills_grid.partial_chip: bg-warning-subtle text-warning border border-warning-border rounded-full px-3 py-1 body_sm font-medium
skills_grid.missing_chip: bg-danger-subtle text-danger border border-danger-border rounded-full px-3 py-1 body_sm opacity-60 line-through

screens.name: AI Explainability
screens.purpose: Full AI reasoning trace. Every score has a specific, auditable justification.
screens.breathing_notes: This is a reading experience. Wide margins, generous line-height, nothing competing for attention.
screens.back_nav: ChevronLeft + 'Back to Priya Sharma' — body_sm text-muted

content.heading: h2 mt-4 — 'We recommend moving forward with Priya.'
content.reasoning: body_lg text-secondary leading-relaxed mt-2 — one-sentence AI rationale

confidence_bar.label: Label — 'AI CONFIDENCE'
confidence_bar.bar: flex-1 h-2 rounded-full bg-border-default — inner fill animated 91% width in success color
confidence_bar.note: body_sm text-muted mt-2 — 'Based on data completeness, signal strength, and resume consistency.'

positive_card.header: flex items-center gap-2 — CheckCircle2 text-success + h3 text-success 'Positive signals'
positive_card.items: mt-4 space-y-3 — each: flex items-start gap-2 — CheckCircle2 14px text-success mt-0.5 + body_sm text-secondary

concern_card.header: flex items-center gap-2 — AlertCircle text-warning + h3 text-warning 'Areas to explore'
concern_card.items: mt-4 space-y-3 — each: flex items-start gap-2 — AlertCircle 14px text-warning mt-0.5 + body_sm text-secondary

missing_skills.header: flex items-center gap-2 mb-4 — XCircle text-danger + h3 'Missing or unverified skills'

reasoning_timeline.header: h2 mb-6 — 'How we scored this candidate'

left.indicator: 36px rounded-full bg-primary-subtle border-2 border-primary flex items-center justify-center — icon 16px text-primary

screens.name: Candidate Comparison
screens.purpose: Side-by-side comparison of 2–3 candidates. Helps the recruiter make the final call.
screens.breathing_notes: Wide table with pinned label column. Rows are grouped into sections with breathing room between groups.

header.heading: h1 — 'Compare candidates'
header.subtext: body_md text-muted mt-1 — 'Comparing 2 of 3 max candidates'

comparison_table.type: CSS Grid — 1 label col + 2–3 candidate cols

candidate_header.close_button: X in top-right corner of each column

add_candidate_col.style: border-2 border-dashed border-default rounded-lg h-[100px] flex items-center justify-center cursor-pointer hover:border-primary hover:bg-primary-subtle/20 transition-all

rows.label: Capability radar
rows.type: Overlapping RadarChart — both candidates on same chart

row_style.group_header_row: bg-surface px-6 py-3 — label text-muted sticky-ish

comparison_table.winner_highlight: Column with highest overall score gets left/right border in primary color and header glow

sticky_action_bar.position: sticky bottom-0 bg-surface border-t border-default py-4 px-6 flex justify-between items-center
sticky_action_bar.left: body_sm text-muted — 'Comparing: Priya Sharma vs Ravi Kumar'
sticky_action_bar.right: flex gap-3 — [Export comparison PDF — outline] [Shortlist top candidate — primary]

screens.name: Analytics
screens.purpose: Organization-level intelligence. Trends, gaps, and hiring health.
screens.breathing_notes: Widget grid with 24px gutters. Each widget is self-contained with its own header. No clutter.

page_header.heading: h1 — 'Analytics'
page_header.subtext: body_md text-muted mt-1 — 'Insights across all 38 jobs · Last updated 2 minutes ago'

widgets.title: Hiring funnel
widgets.subtext: Conversion at each stage across all active jobs
widgets.color: Gradient from primary to secondary (top to bottom)

widgets.title: Score distribution
widgets.subtext: How candidates scored across all rankings
widgets.bar_colors: Mapped by score range using score_spectrum tokens

widgets.title: Top skill gaps
widgets.subtext: Most frequently missing required skills across all jobs
widgets.bar_color: Gradient danger→warning based on gap severity

widgets.title: Profile integrity
widgets.subtext: Breakdown of candidate profile reliability scores

widgets.title: Skill gap by role
widgets.subtext: Required skill coverage compared to candidate averages

ScoreBadge.md: px-3 py-1 text-sm rounded-full font-medium — default

KPICard.props: { label, value, icon, color, trend?, trendDir?, tooltip? }
KPICard.hover: elevation level_1 → level_2 + border-color transitions to primary/25

ProgressRing.props: { value: number; size: number; strokeWidth?: number; color?: string; animated?: boolean; showLabel?: boolean }
ProgressRing.implementation: SVG circle — circumference = 2πr — strokeDashoffset = circumference * (1 - value/100)
ProgressRing.animation: CSS transition on strokeDashoffset with duration_data easing_default

UploadZone.props: { onFilesAccepted, maxFiles?: number, maxSizeMB?: number, accept?: string[] }

FilterDrawer.animation: Framer Motion — x: '100%' → x: 0 over 300ms easing_default
FilterDrawer.overlay: bg-black/50 backdrop-blur-sm — closes drawer on click

CommandPalette.style: Fixed centered modal, max-width 540px, bg-surface border border-default rounded-2xl shadow-level_4

useCandidateStore.state: { candidates, filters, sortBy, sortDir, selectedIds, compareList }

react_query.stale_time: 300000ms (5 min) for analytics, 60000ms (1 min) for candidate lists
react_query.loading_strategy: Always show skeleton UI — never block entire page for loading

ranking.page.tsx: AI Ranking entry — JD + upload form

src/lib.constants.ts: Score thresholds, color map fn, dimension weights

src/hooks.useUpload.ts: Handles file selection, validation, upload progress

typescript_interfaces.Candidate: { id: string; name: string; email?: string; phone?: string; location: string; linkedinUrl?: string; currentTitle: string; currentCompany: string; experienceYears: number; availability: 'immediate' | 'within_30d' | 'within_90d' | 'unavailable'; overallScore: number; grade: 'A+' | 'A' | 'B+' | 'B' | 'C' | 'D'; recommendation: 'highly_recommended' | 'recommended' | 'consider' | 'not_recommended'; dimensions: ScoreDimensions; skillsMatched: string[]; skillsMissing: string[]; skillsPartial: string[]; positiveSignals: Signal[]; concerns: Signal[]; aiSummary: string; confidence: number; reasoning: ReasoningStep[]; experience: ExperienceItem[]; education: EducationItem[]; certifications: Certification[]; }
typescript_interfaces.ScoreDimensions: { semanticMatch: number; careerGrowth: number; hiringReadiness: number; profileIntegrity: number; leadershipSignal: number; skillCoverage: number; }
typescript_interfaces.ReasoningStep: { dimension: string; icon: string; heading: string; reasoning: string; scoreContribution: number; }
typescript_interfaces.Job: { id: string; title: string; company?: string; description: string; requiredSkills: string[]; niceToHaveSkills: string[]; experienceMin: number; experienceMax?: number; createdAt: string; status: 'draft' | 'processing' | 'complete' | 'error'; candidateCount?: number; avgScore?: number; }
typescript_interfaces.AnalyticsData: { hiringFunnel: FunnelStage[]; scoreDistribution: ScoreBucket[]; topSkillGaps: SkillGap[]; avgHiringReadiness: number; readinessTrend: TrendPoint[]; integrityDistribution: PieSegment[]; skillGapByRole: GroupedBar[]; }

candidates.name: Priya Sharma
candidates.aiSummary: Priya is an exceptionally strong match. Her 6 years across two high-growth B2B SaaS companies maps directly to your tech stack. She was promoted to Senior in under 3 years — well above average. Two gaps worth exploring in interview: Kubernetes operations and system design at scale.

candidates.name: Rahul Menon
candidates.aiSummary: Rahul is a solid match available immediately. His 4 years at Razorpay — a high-scale fintech — signals comfort with production systems. Key gaps are cloud infrastructure and containerization, which could be bridged with 1–2 months of onboarding.

candidates.name: Ananya Krishnan
candidates.aiSummary: Ananya shows strong growth trajectory for her 3 years of experience, but the current skill overlap is too low for a senior role. She would be worth interviewing if you have a structured onboarding plan and can accommodate a 6–12 month ramp-up period.

accessibility.focus: All interactive elements have visible focus ring: outline: 2px solid #2563EB; outline-offset: 2px;
accessibility.color_contrast: All text on dark backgrounds meets 4.5:1. Score chips are tested for legibility.

aria.score_ring: role='img' aria-label='Overall AI score: 87 out of 100'
aria.table: role='table' with proper thead, tbody, th[scope='col'], td semantics

keyboard.tab_order: Logical left-to-right, top-to-bottom throughout all screens
keyboard.esc: Close modals, drawers, dropdowns, command palette

performance_targets.table_virtualization: TanStack Virtual for tables with > 50 rows
performance_targets.code_splitting: Each screen is a separate dynamic import — next/dynamic
performance_targets.bundle_size: No chart library imported globally — Recharts imported per chart component only

phase_1_foundation: Install and configure: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, shadcn/ui, Framer Motion, Zustand, React Query
Set up design tokens in tailwind.config.ts (all color tokens as CSS variables)
Build layout shell: Sidebar + Header + PageWrapper
Build atom components: ScoreBadge, RecommendationBadge, ProgressRing, KPICard, StatusDot
phase_2_core_screens: S1: Dashboard — JD input + UploadZone + Recent jobs
S3: Ranking — CandidateTable with TanStack Table + FilterDrawer
phase_3_deep_features: S4: Candidate Profile — ScoreRing (custom SVG) + RadarChart + Explainability tab
S5: Explainability — verdict banner + signals + reasoning timeline
phase_4_intelligence: Export: PDF via @react-pdf/renderer, CSV via papaparse

breathing_space: Every card has at least 28px internal padding
Section gaps are never less than 40px
Table rows are never less than 72px tall
No two cards touch — minimum 20px gap everywhere
real_world_completeness: Every list has an empty state with an action
Every form field has validation and error state
Every action has a loading state
Every destructive action has a confirmation dialog
Upload shows real progress (file count + bytes)
All scores have visible explanations accessible in ≤2 clicks
Pagination works and preserves filters + sort
Sidebar collapse is smooth and remembers state in localStorage
Bulk action bar only appears when rows are selected
Score ring animates on every mount — no static circles
All toast notifications auto-dismiss after 4s with manual close option
All table columns are sortable on click
Mobile layout works at 375px with bottom navigation replacing sidebar
